purpose: Keep Claude Code and Codex behavior aligned without forcing incompatible config files to be identical.
principles: Use shared instruction sources where possible.
Pair hooks when both tools expose compatible lifecycle behavior.
Keep mutable or secret-bearing settings live, and track only public-safe templates.
Document intentional divergence instead of leaving unexplained drift.

intentional_differences.reason: Claude settings are hierarchical and mutable; keep a public-safe template instead of symlinking live settings.
Codex config.toml contains local paths, provider choices, MCP auth headers, and hook trust state; keep a redacted public template only.
Claude has Notification, Elicitation, and PermissionRequest hooks that do not map cleanly to the current Codex hook surface.

rules/common/*.md (development workflow, testing, coding style, etc.) is loaded only via ~/.claude/rules/common; Codex has no equivalent rules directory and relies on home/AGENTS.md alone. Intentional for now — revisit if Codex sessions need the same standards.
Claude keeps only code-reviewer, security-reviewer, and build-error-resolver active. Built-in Plan/Explore and skills cover the retired planner, database, E2E, and communication roles.

Claude agents are trimmed to the small always-available roster, while Codex keeps language and execution subagent TOML files with explicit model pins. The extra Codex agents are dormant until selected and do not add Claude context.
